what_it_does: Implements the Vi assistant UI surface: a context-store pub/sub, an imperative DOM highlight overlay, an orbital trigger button, an expandable chat panel with situational awareness, suggestion chips, makeable-specimen previews, guided stream launching, and a voice-mode overlay. It reads project context and brain data from window registries and renders React components assigned to window.*.
observations: Heavy use of window globals: MS_VI_BRAIN, MS_DATA, MS_STREAMS, MS_RESOLVE, MS_LAUNCH, useViChat, StreamRunner, StreamLauncher, KindSpecimen, Icon, ViMark, ViThread, ViMemoryButton.
Components are exported by assigning to window (window.ViOrbit, window.ViPanel, window.ViVoice).
ContextStore is a hand-rolled pub/sub attached to window.ViContext.
Highlight overlay imperatively creates/appends a fixed div and positions it from getBoundingClientRect.
Chat replies are driven by window.useViChat and grounded in a 'brain' object.
Suggestions, makeables, streams-here, knows, and look all come from brain or fall back to MS_DATA.
Voice mode uses Web Speech API with lang 'en-AU' and shows a decorative gold orb, but transcript is only displayed, not sent.
In-panel guided streams are rendered with StreamRunner when a vi-run-stream event fires.
Model label in the panel says 'Claude Sonnet 4.6' while the header comment says 'Haiku 4.5'.
Some footer buttons ('Reference', 'Act on UI') have no onClick handlers.
standards: Tight integration with a unified 'brain' (MS_VI_BRAIN) that supplies context, suggestions, makeables, streams, and memory.
Makeable items are visualized with KindSpecimen previews using resolved instance values and variant bias.
Guided streams can run in-place inside the panel via StreamRunner.
Voice overlay presents a realtime-style listening orb even though it only uses browser speech recognition.
Imperative highlight pointer lets Vi visually point at DOM elements.
conventions: Global registration via window.* for cross-bundle access.
Pub/sub context store for shared mutable state.
Event-based communication: vi-ask and vi-run-stream window events.
CSS class namespace 'vi-*' and BEM-ish modifiers.
Defensive fallbacks to window.MS_DATA when brain is absent.
Imperative DOM manipulation for highlight overlay rather than React portal.
concerns: Several globals are dereferenced inside render/map without guards (e.g., window.MS_STREAMS.get, window.MS_RESOLVE.instance, window.StreamRunner).
Voice state ('listening/thinking/speaking') is declared but never advanced by recognition results; transcript is never submitted to chat.
Highlight overlay element is created once but not removed on clear, only faded.
Mismatch between header comment model (Haiku 4.5) and rendered label (Claude Sonnet 4.6).
Footer buttons 'Reference' and 'Act on UI' are wired visually but have no behavior.
useViContext subscribes to updates but does not unsubscribe if the component unmounts in a non-React-tree scenario.
notes: This is the React UI shell for Vi, the project's context-aware assistant, wiring chat, voice, guided streams, and a highlight pointer through a shared brain and several window registries.
questions: What is the exact contract/API of MS_VI_BRAIN?
Where is window.useViChat defined and how does it interact with window.claude.complete?
Are StreamRunner, StreamLauncher, KindSpecimen, ViThread, and ViMemoryButton defined in the same bundle or injected elsewhere?
How is the voice transcript intended to be submitted to the chat backend?
What is the intended behavior of the 'Act on UI' and 'Reference' tools?
How is ViMemory persisted and loaded?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file builds the Vi assistant UI with chat, context awareness, voice, suggestions, makeables, guided streams, and a DOM highlight pointer, as described in the header comment.
apparent_themes: Progressive disclosure from orb to panel to voice/stream
intention_signals: Create a persistent, context-aware AI assistant embedded in the design studio that can answer questions, suggest next actions, generate artifacts, run guided workflows, and visually point out interface elements to the user.
novelty: Combines an orbital always-on trigger, a unified brain registry, makeable-specimen previews, in-panel guided stream execution, and an imperative DOM highlight pointer for 'teaching' moments.
contribution: Contributes the Vi assistant UI shell plus the context-store and highlight-overlay primitives, and demonstrates integration patterns for brain-driven suggestions, makeables, streams, and voice.
summary_for_embedding: A React module that renders the Vi assistant's orbital trigger, expandable chat panel, voice overlay, DOM highlight pointer, and context-aware suggestion/makeable/stream surfaces by reading from window registries such as MS_VI_BRAIN, MS_DATA, MS_STREAMS, and MS_RESOLVE.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to provide an ever-present, context-aware AI assistant interface inside the Studio that can chat, suggest actions, create artifacts, run guided workflows, and visually point out UI elements.

symbols.does: Subscribes to the window.ViContext pub/sub and returns the current assistant context as React state.
symbols.description: A custom React hook that initializes state from window.ViContext.get() and registers setCtx as a subscriber, so any part of the studio that updates ViContext re-renders components using this hook.

symbols.does: Listens for a custom vi-ask window event and forwards its detail to the chat send function.
symbols.description: An event handler registered inside ViPanel that converts vi-ask events into chat messages, enabling external UI surfaces to ask Vi a question without directly controlling the panel's input state.

symbols.does: Listens for a custom vi-run-stream event and opens the matching guided stream inside the panel.
symbols.description: An event handler that checks whether the event's stream id exists in window.MS_STREAMS and, if so, sets the panel's stream state so StreamRunner renders the guided sequence in place.

symbols.does: Maps a workspace id to a human-readable label using window.MS_DATA.workspaces.
symbols.description: A helper that looks up the workspace list in MS_DATA and returns the matching workspace label, falling back to the raw id if no match is found.

symbols.does: Maps shorthand suggestion icon names to the actual Icon component names used by the UI.
symbols.description: A small lookup helper that normalizes suggestion icon identifiers (e.g., 'path' to 'path-flow', 'compare' to 'compare') and defaults to 'sparkle' for unknown values.
